hero.headline: Orchestrate all your <hi>AI API traffic</hi>
hero.subheadline: API key distribution, multi-upstream routing, load balancing with circuit breaking, and per-request billing — manage every bit of AI traffic from one self-hosted console.

terminal.caption: Point one base URL at the gateway — AutoRouter handles routing, balancing, and billing.

features.title: Core capabilities
features.subtitle: Built for multi-upstream, highly available AI traffic distribution

routing.title: Capability-based routing
routing.desc: Detects capability from the request path and model, then routes traffic to the matching upstream.

balancing.title: Balancing & circuit breaking
balancing.desc: Distributes by priority and weight, trips unhealthy upstreams, and fails over to the next candidate.

quota.title: Quota & concurrency control
quota.desc: Applies quota, concurrency, and queue-admission limits per key to keep upstreams stable.

billing.title: Per-request billing
billing.desc: Computes a billing snapshot for every request from synced prices, overrides, and multipliers.

keys.title: API key distribution
keys.desc: Issues and manages client keys, scoped to authorized upstreams and expiry.

failover.title: Failover & session affinity
failover.desc: Switches candidates seamlessly on failure and can pin a conversation to a chosen upstream.

cta.title: Ready to connect?
cta.desc: Sign in to the console and finish key issuance and upstream setup in minutes.

nav.logoutConfirm: Are you sure you want to log out?

users.managementDesc: Create and manage user accounts and their access
users.createUserDesc: Provision a login account. The user signs in with the username and password you set.
users.editUserDesc: Update the display name, role, or active state.
users.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
users.userNotFoundHint: This user may have been deleted, or the link is invalid.
users.changeUsernameDesc: Set a new login username for this user.
users.resetPasswordDesc: Set a new password for this user. They will use it on next login.
users.configureUpstreamsDesc: Choose which upstreams this user's keys may route to.
users.assignKeysDesc: Assign existing API keys to this user as their owner.
users.deleteUserConfirm: Delete user "{username}"? Their API keys will be detached but kept. This cannot be undone.
users.lastAdminHint: Cannot disable or delete the last active administrator
users.formValidationFailed: Please check the form for errors

repository.title: Project Repository
repository.description: Browse source code, releases, and issue tracking
repository.open: Open the project repository on GitHub

auth.title: AUTOROUTER
auth.subtitle: Admin Console
auth.tokenInfo: Admin Token is generated by the system administrator during server configuration.
auth.accountAuthRequired: Sign in with the account provisioned by your administrator
auth.tooManyAttempts: Too many login attempts. Please try again later.
auth.accountInfo: Your account is provisioned by the system administrator. Contact them to reset your password.

dashboard.title: System Monitor
dashboard.controlPanelDesc: Manage API keys and upstream services
dashboard.manageApiKeysDesc: Create, edit or revoke client access keys
dashboard.configureUpstreamsDesc: Add or modify AI service providers

topology.title: ROUTING TOPOLOGY
topology.desc: Live routing topology from the gateway to upstream providers

livePulse.label: Live pulse
livePulse.statusLiveTooltip: Receiving gateway status in real time
livePulse.statusConnectingTooltip: Establishing live connection to the gateway
livePulse.statusFallbackTooltip: Live connection unavailable, switched to periodic refresh

keys.title: API Keys
keys.managementDesc: Create and manage API Keys for client access
keys.createKeyDesc: Create a new API Key for client access, select available Upstreams
keys.keyDescriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of this Key's purpose...
keys.upstreamAccessModeDesc: Use unrestricted access by default and only choose specific upstreams when needed.
keys.unrestrictedAccessDesc: Allow this Key to access every available upstream.
keys.restrictedAccessDesc: Only allow this Key to access the upstreams selected below.
keys.selectUpstreamsDesc: Which upstream services can this Key access
keys.searchUpstreams: Search upstreams by name, provider, or description...
keys.noMatchingUpstreams: No upstreams match the current search
keys.allowedModelsDesc: Leave empty to allow every model. Import candidates from the upstreams this Key can use.
keys.allowedModelsPlaceholder: Add model names, separated by comma or Enter
keys.allowedModelsEmpty: No model limit configured. This Key may request any model that an authorized upstream accepts.
keys.modelCandidatesEmpty: No candidate models are available from accessible upstreams.
keys.allModelCandidatesImported: All candidate models are already in the current limit list.
keys.noMatchingModelCandidates: No candidate models match the current search
keys.noMatchingAllowedModels: No allowed models match the current search
keys.spendingRulesDesc: Configure amount-based limits for this key. Any exceeded rule will block later requests.
keys.quotaLimitPositive: Limit amount must be greater than 0
keys.quotaStatusPending: Quota status is initializing. Refresh in a moment.
keys.formValidationFailed: Please complete required fields before saving.
keys.keyCreatedDesc: Copy this Key now. You won't be able to see the full value again after closing.
keys.revokeKeyDesc: This action cannot be undone. All clients using this Key will immediately receive 401 authentication failures.
keys.revokeKeyWarning: Revoked Keys are kept in the database for audit and are not removed from request logs. To re-enable access, you must create a new Key.
keys.noKeysDesc: Create your first client Key so external apps can call the proxy API via Bearer Token. To limit which upstreams the Key can reach, switch to "Specific Upstreams" at creation time and tick the allowed ones.
keys.noKeysFoundDesc: No API Keys match your search criteria
keys.legacyKey: Legacy key - regenerate to enable reveal
keys.editKeyDesc: Modify API Key name, description, permissions and expiration
keys.keyActiveDesc: Disabled keys cannot access the API

logs.title: Request Logs
logs.managementDesc: View and analyze API request history
logs.focusNotFound: This log was not found - it may already have been pruned.
logs.noLogsDesc: Request logs will appear here once API calls are made
logs.liveStatusLiveDesc: Latest request log status changes are updating automatically
logs.liveStatusFallbackDesc: Live updates are unavailable, so the page switched to short-interval refresh
logs.noMatchingLogsDesc: No logs match your current filter criteria
logs.noLogsInRange: No logs in the selected time range
logs.noLogsInRangeDesc: Switch the time range to ALL to see older history

timelineQueueLifecycle.waiting: The request entered the wait queue and is still waiting for a slot.
timelineQueueLifecycle.resumed: The request waited in queue and then resumed execution.
timelineQueueLifecycle.timed_out: The request stayed in queue until the wait timeout ended the flow.
timelineQueueLifecycle.aborted: The client disconnected while the request was still waiting in queue.

logs.compensationBadgeTooltip: session_id recovered from request body (header was missing)
logs.journeySelectionReasonUnavailable: This log did not record a structured selection reason
logs.journeySelectionReasonAffinityHit: Reused the upstream because session affinity hit the existing binding
logs.journeySelectionReasonAffinityMigrated: Moved the session to a higher-priority upstream because affinity migration applied
logs.journeySelectionReasonWeighted: Selected this upstream by weight among the remaining available candidates
logs.journeySelectionReasonHalfOpen: Sent this request as a half-open probe to verify the recovering upstream
logs.journeySelectionReasonSingleCandidate: Selected this upstream because only one candidate remained after filtering
logs.journeyRetryReasonText: Retried the current upstream after {upstream} failed with {reason}

journeyQueueLifecycle.waiting: Queued and still waiting for an available slot
journeyQueueLifecycle.resumed: Queued first, then resumed and continued execution
journeyQueueLifecycle.timed_out: Queued first, then timed out before any upstream request was sent
journeyQueueLifecycle.aborted: Queued first, then stopped because the client disconnected

logs.journeyRequestQueuedResumed: Resumed after waiting in the upstream queue
logs.journeyRequestQueuedTimedOut: Timed out while waiting in the upstream queue
logs.journeyRequestQueuedAborted: Aborted while waiting in the upstream queue
logs.journeyRequestSentTo: Sent request to the following upstream
logs.journeyRequestNotSent: Did not send the request upstream; the flow ended inside the gateway

upstreams.title: Upstreams
upstreams.managementDesc: Configure and manage upstream AI service providers
upstreams.createUpstreamDesc: Configure a new upstream AI service provider
upstreams.deleteUpstreamDesc: This action cannot be undone. All client API Keys bound to this upstream will immediately fail to route to the corresponding models.
upstreams.deleteUpstreamWarning: Circuit breaker state, statistics, and request logs are preserved for audit, but the upstream itself must be recreated from scratch to restore service.
upstreams.noUpstreamsDesc: Add your first OpenAI-compatible upstream (or register a CLIProxyAPI instance) before client API Keys can route to any model.
upstreams.noFilteredUpstreamsDesc: Adjust or reset filters to view matching upstreams
upstreams.probeDiagnosticsDescription: Runs a real protocol-aware request with saved settings. Results are diagnostic only and do not affect routing.
upstreams.probeNoSupportedCapability: This upstream has no saved request capability that supports probing.
upstreams.probeSaveBeforeRun: Save current configuration changes before probing to avoid testing stale settings.
upstreams.probeUpstreamResponsePreview: The response is long. Showing the first {count} characters by default; expand to view the full content.
upstreams.probeUpstreamResponseFullHint: Full response is shown; scroll inside the response box to view the end when content is long.
upstreams.probeUpstreamResponseEmpty: No upstream response content was captured for this probe.
upstreams.priorityDescription: Lower number = higher priority. Tier 0 is tried first, then tier 1, etc.
upstreams.weightDescription: Higher weight = more requests routed to this upstream within the same tier
upstreams.unsavedChangesDescription: Your edits are not saved yet. Leaving now will discard all changes in this form.
upstreams.billingInputMultiplierDesc: Applied to input token cost for this upstream (0-100)
upstreams.billingOutputMultiplierDesc: Applied to output token cost for this upstream (0-100)
upstreams.noSpendingRules: No spending rules configured. Upstream has unlimited spending.
upstreams.spendingLimitDesc: Maximum spending allowed per period. Leave empty for unlimited.
upstreams.spendingLimitMustBePositive: Spending limit must be greater than 0
upstreams.spendingPeriodTypeDesc: Time window for spending limit reset.
upstreams.spendingPeriodHoursDesc: Duration of rolling window in hours (1-8760). Defaults to 24 when left empty.
upstreams.formValidationFailed: Please complete required fields before saving.
upstreams.catalogSavedConfigHint: Refresh uses current form values and applies after save.
upstreams.modelDiscoverySectionDescription: Choose how this upstream exposes its model list and preview the exact discovery request.
upstreams.customDiscoveryEndpointDescription: Relative paths resolve from the normalized API root. Absolute URLs are used as-is.
upstreams.modelRulesSectionDescription: {count} explicit rule(s). Requests stay open only when no explicit rule exists.
upstreams.modelRuleTypeDescription_exact: Allow only the exact model name shown here.
upstreams.modelRuleTypeDescription_regex: Allow any model that matches this regular expression.
upstreams.modelRuleTypeDescription_alias: Rewrite the requested model name before forwarding upstream.
upstreams.modelRuleAliasTargetDescription: Forward requests with the target model after the alias matches.
upstreams.modelRuleManualHint: Manual rules can be edited freely and take effect after saving this form.
upstreams.modelRuleImportedHint: Imported rules can be refined here. Editing them turns the rule into a manual rule.
upstreams.catalogSectionDescription: Scan the cached catalog quickly, then import only the candidates that should become explicit rules.
upstreams.catalogEmptyState: No catalog yet. Refresh to load models.
upstreams.providerTypeDescription: Determines authentication method and auto-routing (e.g., claude-* routes to anthropic)
upstreams.routeCapabilitiesDescription: Path-first routing will only use selected capabilities. One upstream can enable multiple capabilities.
upstreams.routeCapabilitiesSingleProvider: Selected route capabilities must belong to one provider
upstreams.compatibilityRoutingDesc: Provider type and model rules are retained for fallback compatibility during migration.
upstreams.capabilityClaudeCodeMessagesDesc: POST /v1/messages with Claude Code CLI headers
upstreams.allowedModelsPlaceholder: Enter model name and press Enter, e.g., gpt-4
upstreams.allowedModelsDescription: Leave empty to allow all models
upstreams.modelRedirectsDescription: Map requested model names to upstream-supported model names
upstreams.failureThresholdDesc: Consecutive failures to trigger circuit open (1-100)
upstreams.successThresholdDesc: Consecutive successes to close circuit from half-open (1-100)
upstreams.openDurationDesc: Duration to keep circuit open, in seconds (1-300). Example: 300 = 5 minutes
upstreams.probeIntervalDesc: Probe interval in half-open state, in seconds (1-60). Example: 60 = 1 minute
upstreams.firstByteTimeoutDesc: Maximum time to wait for the first content-bearing stream event before retrying another upstream.
upstreams.streamIdleTimeoutDesc: Maximum idle time allowed between upstream stream chunks after output starts.
upstreams.useGlobalFailureRulesDesc: Apply global ignore rules before recording circuit breaker failures for this upstream.
upstreams.localFailureRulesCreateModeHint: Save the upstream first, then add local failure rules.
upstreams.failureRuleSearchPlaceholder: Search rule name, status, error type, or regex
upstreams.failureRuleNoSearchResults: No failure rules match the search.
upstreams.failureRuleRegexSamplePlaceholder: Enter sample text to test the current regex
upstreams.failureRuleRegexPreviewMatched: The current regex matches the sample text.
upstreams.failureRuleRegexPreviewNotMatched: The current regex does not match the sample text.
upstreams.failureRuleRegexPreviewHint: Fill in a regex and sample text to see the match result.
upstreams.failureRuleErrorTypeUnknownTooltip: This error type is no longer in the supported enum and will never match any upstream event. Remove or replace it.
upstreams.affinityMigrationEnabledDesc: Allow sessions to migrate from lower priority upstreams to this upstream
upstreams.affinityMigrationMetricDesc: Select the metric used to evaluate conversation size
upstreams.affinityMigrationThresholdTokensDesc: Sessions below this token count can migrate (default: 50000)
upstreams.affinityMigrationThresholdLengthDesc: Sessions below this byte count can migrate (default: 51200)
upstreams.officialWebsiteUrlDesc: Optional. If configured, a direct website shortcut will appear in the upstream list.
upstreams.maxConcurrencyDesc: When full, requests are transferred to other available upstreams without opening circuit breaker.
upstreams.queuePolicyEnabledDesc: When every eligible upstream is busy, requests can wait for a released slot on this upstream.
upstreams.queuePolicyDisabledDesc: Requests still transfer or fail immediately when concurrency is full.
upstreams.queuePolicyTimeoutDesc: Maximum time in milliseconds that a request can wait before timing out.
upstreams.queuePolicyMaxQueueLengthPlaceholder: Leave empty for unlimited queue length
upstreams.queuePolicyMaxQueueLengthDesc: Optional upper bound for waiting requests on this upstream.
upstreams.baseUrlAutoAppendV1Hint: The system auto-appended /v1 based on selected capabilities. Requests will be forwarded using this normalized endpoint.
upstreams.baseUrlDuplicateV1Warning: Your base URL already contains /v1. The system will prevent duplicated /v1 segments.
upstreams.finalRequestPreviewEmpty: Enter a valid base URL to preview the final request address
upstreams.finalRequestPreviewHint: The preview updates in real time after capability and base URL changes.
upstreams.workbenchDesc: Review tiered runtime signals, capacity, and quota status, then execute high-frequency actions quickly.
upstreams.workbenchSearchPlaceholder: Search by upstream name or base URL

errors.networkError: Network error, please check your connection
errors.serverError: Server error, please try again later

billing.title: Billing
billing.managementDesc: Maintain model price catalog, fix missing prices, and keep billing consistent
billing.unresolvedDesc: Models without available prices. Add manual overrides to unblock billing.
billing.priceCatalogDesc: Query synced model prices currently used by billing
billing.priceCatalogOverrideHint: Manual overrides take precedence over synced prices
billing.priceCatalogSearchPlaceholder: Search by model name, e.g. gpt-4.1
billing.priceCatalogResetDialogDesc: This will delete manual overrides for {count} model(s) and billing will fall back to synced prices.
billing.priceCatalogResetDialogWarningNoOfficial: Warning: {count} selected model(s) have no synced price. After deletion, they will become unbillable until you re-enter a manual price or the price catalog is synced.
billing.manualEntryDesc: Add or update any model price directly, even if it has not appeared in unresolved list.
billing.manualOverrideResetWarningNoOfficial: {count} model(s) currently have no official price and will become unbillable after removal
billing.logsDesc: Request-level costs are shown in Request Logs (with billing status and unbillable reason).
billing.tierRulesDesc: Models that charge different rates based on context length. Rules from litellm sync are read-only; manual rules can be added.
billing.tierRulesDeleteConfirmDesc: Delete this manual tier rule? This cannot be undone.
billing.tierRulesDuplicateThresholdError: A manual tier rule with the same threshold already exists for this model

backgroundSync.title: Background Tasks
backgroundSync.panelDescription: Review scheduled sync jobs, update scheduling settings, and trigger a single immediate run when needed.
backgroundSync.taskBillingPriceCatalogSyncDesc: Syncs model price data for request cost and tiered pricing.
backgroundSync.taskUpstreamModelCatalogSyncDesc: Refreshes model list caches for opted-in upstreams.
backgroundSync.taskTrafficRecordingCleanupDesc: Deletes expired recording files and indexes by the configured retention window.

trafficRecording.title: Traffic Recording
trafficRecording.settingsDescription: Control request recording in real time and manage stored recording files.
trafficRecording.description: Control proxy request recording, inspect disk usage, and search, view, or delete saved recording details.
trafficRecording.settingsSaveFailed: Failed to save traffic recording settings: {message}
trafficRecording.logSectionIdle: Expand this row to load the matching recording.
trafficRecording.logSectionMissingFile: The recording index exists but the fixture file is missing.

upstreamFailureRules.title: Global Failure Rules
upstreamFailureRules.settingsDescription: Manage shared ignore rules for upstream circuit breaker failures.
upstreamFailureRules.description: Global rules are checked before recording upstream circuit breaker failures for upstreams that use global failure rules.
upstreamFailureRules.editorDescription: Add shared rules for status codes, error types, response bodies, or response headers.

compensation.title: Header Compensation
compensation.managementDesc: Configure outbound header compensation rules for upstream requests
compensation.builtinRulesDesc: System default rules. You can enable or disable them, but you cannot edit or delete them.
compensation.builtinRulesMissing: Built-in rules not found (migration may be incomplete).
compensation.customRulesDesc: Rules you created. You can edit or delete them.
compensation.deleteRuleDesc: Are you sure you want to delete this rule?
compensation.sourcesRequired: At least one source is required
compensation.capabilityMatrixDesc: Which capabilities each rule applies to

cliproxy.pageDescription: Manage CLIProxyAPI instances and CLI OAuth upstreams
cliproxy.noInstances: No CLIProxyAPI instances yet. After enabling the cliproxyapi sidecar in docker-compose, click the button above to register its address and management key so Codex / Claude / Gemini OAuth upstreams become available.
cliproxy.fieldModeHint: Managed sidecar runs in the same docker-compose as AutoRouter; External points to a separately deployed CLIProxyAPI.
cliproxy.fieldBaseUrlHint: Address AutoRouter uses to forward proxy requests. When AutoRouter and CLIProxyAPI share a Docker network, use the container service name (e.g. http://cliproxyapi:8317), not localhost.
cliproxy.fieldManagementUrlHint: Address used to call the CLIProxyAPI management API, usually the same as the proxy URL. In a Docker network, use the service name rather than localhost.
cliproxy.fieldClientApiKeyHint: Must match the api-keys configured in CLIProxyAPI
cliproxy.fieldClientApiKeyEditHint: Leave blank to keep the current key
cliproxy.fieldManagementKeyEditHint: Leave blank to keep the current key
cliproxy.testStatus_unreachable_hint: If AutoRouter and CLIProxyAPI share a Docker network, use the container service name instead of localhost.
cliproxy.testBeforeSave: Run a connectivity pre-check with the values above.
cliproxy.deleteInstanceWarning: Deleting an instance does not affect the CLIProxyAPI service itself.
cliproxy.selectInstanceHint: Select an instance above to view its OAuth accounts.
cliproxy.noAccounts: No OAuth accounts yet. Click "OAuth Login" to bind your first Codex / Claude / Gemini account, or click "Sync Accounts" to pull existing logins from CLIProxyAPI.
cliproxy.fieldPrefixHint: Used for fixed-account routing via a model name prefix
cliproxy.fieldProxyUrlHint: Per-account outbound proxy used by this OAuth account
cliproxy.oauthLoginDescription: Pick a provider, click "Start Login" to obtain the authorization URL, and complete login in your browser. This dialog auto-detects success and refreshes the account list — no manual sync needed.
cliproxy.oauthSelectProvider: Select a provider to log in with.
cliproxy.oauthLoginTimeout: Login timed out. Start the login again.
cliproxy.poolUpstreamDialogDescription: Create a pool upstream for the selected provider. A pool upstream aggregates every OAuth account of that provider under this instance and dispatches requests across them by the built-in strategy — no need to create one upstream per account.
cliproxy.accountUpstreamDialogDescription: Create an upstream bound to this OAuth account.
cliproxy.uploadAuthFileDescription: Upload an OAuth auth file in JSON format. After upload, the account list is automatically synced.
cliproxy.uploadAuthFileChoose: Click to select a .json file or drop one here
cliproxy.deleteAuthFileDescription: Delete this auth file from CLIProxyAPI and remove its local cache. This action cannot be undone.
cliproxy.accountModelsEmpty: No models available for this account
cliproxy.oauthManualCallbackDescription: If the automatic callback can't reach AutoRouter, paste the callback URL from your browser address bar here to complete the login.

overview.todayRequestsHint: Requests made by your keys today

requests.managementDesc: Request records produced by your own API keys

keys.managementDesc: Create and manage API keys owned by you
keys.dialogDesc: Keys created here are owned by you and limited to the upstreams your administrator has granted.
keys.upstreamsDesc: Choose from the upstreams granted to you by the administrator.
keys.noGrantedUpstreams: No upstreams have been granted to you yet. Contact your administrator.
keys.spendingRulesTightenHint: Spending rules can only be tightened: existing rules cannot be removed and limits cannot be raised.
keys.noKeysDesc: Create your first API key to call the proxy API. Keys are limited to the upstreams granted to you.
keys.disabledByAdminHint: An administrator disabled this key. Contact your administrator to restore it.

password.cardDesc: Enter your current password and choose a new one of at least 8 characters.
password.newTooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters
password.confirmMismatch: The two passwords do not match
